metadata.source: Grade 3 English final writing and language batch

subject: English
skill: Informative Writing With Facts and Details

lesson.story_mission: Join the Grade 3 Writing Studio team to practice informative writing with facts and details.
lesson.objective: I can use Grade 3 strategies for informative writing with facts and details.
lesson.concept_explanation: Strong Grade 3 writers plan the parts of informative writing with facts and details, choose words that fit, and reread so readers can understand the message.
lesson.common_misconception: A writer may add opinions or fun sentences instead of facts that teach the topic.
lesson.real_world_connection: Clear writing helps classmates, families, and teachers understand your ideas in every subject.
lesson.lesson_summary: Plan the parts, write complete sentences, and reread with the checklist.
lesson.mini_lesson: Read the prompt, name the writing job, build the sentence or paragraph, then check your conventions.

worked_examples.text: Watch how a writer uses the model to complete informative writing with facts and details before answering.

question_audio.text: Watch how a writer uses the model to complete informative writing with facts and details before answering.
question_audio.label: Read Question

hint_ladder: Read the prompt and name the writing job.
Use the visual model to plan the missing part.
Reread for capitals, punctuation, and complete meaning.

guided_practice.prompt: Which topic sentence introduces an informative paragraph about honeybees?
guided_practice.correct_answer: Honeybees are interesting to learn about.
hints: Read the prompt and identify the writing job.
Use the visual model to plan your answer.
Reread your answer before you finish.
guided_practice.explanation: This answer matches the Grade 3 writing target and gives the reader enough information.
guided_practice.feedback: Strong writing move — you used the checklist.

checkpoint.prompt: Write an informative paragraph about owls with a topic sentence, facts, details, linking words, and a conclusion.
checkpoint.correct_answer: Owls are interesting to learn about. First, owls are birds. Also, many owls hunt at night. Now you know an important fact about owls.
hints: Read the prompt and identify the writing job.
Use the visual model to plan your answer.
Reread your answer before you finish.
checkpoint.explanation: This answer matches the Grade 3 writing target and gives the reader enough information.
checkpoint.feedback: Strong writing move — you used the checklist.

acceptable_answers: Frogs are interesting to learn about. First, frogs are amphibians. Also, they begin life as tadpoles. Now you know an important fact about frogs.

audio.text: Frogs are interesting to learn about. First, frogs are amphibians. Also, they begin life as tadpoles. Now you know an important fact about frogs.
audio.label: Listen

facts: Adult frogs can breathe through skin.
details: Adult frogs can breathe through skin.

questions.prompt: Write an informative paragraph about recycling with a topic sentence, facts, details, linking words, and a conclusion.
questions.correct_answer: Recycling are interesting to learn about. First, recycling reuses materials. Also, paper, cans, and bottles can be recycled. Now you know an important fact about recycling.
hints: Read the prompt and identify the writing job.
Use the visual model to plan your answer.
Reread your answer before you finish.
questions.explanation: This answer matches the Grade 3 writing target and gives the reader enough information.

story.text: Welcome to this Grade 3 Writing / Composition mission about Informative Writing With Facts and Details. Listen for the writing goal, notice the visual model, and think about what a reader needs. Press Start Mission when you are ready to plan, answer, and check your work.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Informative Writing With Facts and Details. Look at the model and name each important part before you answer. Use the checklist to check meaning, order, capitalization, punctuation, and complete sentences. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This watch page shows a worked example for Informative Writing With Facts and Details. Step one is to read the prompt and decide the writing job. Step two is to use the model to plan the answer. Step three is to reread the final answer and explain why it fits. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo lets you try Informative Writing With Facts and Details with support. Read the sentence or paragraph task first, then use the model to decide what belongs. Check your answer out loud like a careful Grade 3 writer. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This practice page helps you try Informative Writing With Facts and Details. Press Read Question to hear the exact prompt before you answer. Use the visual model, write or choose a complete response, and check the sentence for meaning. Answer carefully, then continue.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This challenge page asks you to use Informative Writing With Facts and Details with more independence. Press Read Question if you want the prompt read aloud. Think about the purpose, choose details or conventions that fit, and reread before you submit. Answer carefully, then continue.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This checkpoint shows what you can do with Informative Writing With Facts and Details. Press Read Question to hear the task, then use the model and checklist. Check for complete meaning, capitals, punctuation, and any special writing parts. Answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge celebrates your Grade 3 work on Informative Writing With Facts and Details. Think about the strategy you used, such as planning, adding details, using dialogue, combining sentences, or checking punctuation. Press Next to see your profile and keep growing.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth for Informative Writing With Facts and Details. Look at attempts, correct answers, accuracy, hints, and the recommendation. Continue to Skill Practice for focused review, replay the mission, or go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
